description_long: This object is a small, standalone public toilet structure made entirely of wood, featuring a rustic, weathered appearance. The exterior is composed of vertically aligned wooden planks, with a slanted, overhanging roof constructed from matching wooden boards. The front side has an open entrance with a rectangular cutout at the lower center, likely serving as a ventilation or waste outlet. The roof extends well beyond the front, providing some shelter from rain. There are no visible windows, signage, or additional features such as handrails or ramps. The overall design is simple and utilitarian, suitable for placement in parks or rural urban settings.
description: A small, rustic wooden public toilet with a slanted overhanging roof and open front entrance, made from weathered planks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the tall, slanted wooden roof and vertically aligned planks.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the flat, rectangular roof surface made of wooden boards.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the slanted roof and vertical plank construction.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the open entrance and a rectangular cutout at the bottom center.
attribute_ventilation_features: rectangular cutout at the bottom front panel
attribute_roof_structure: Slanted, single-pitch roof made of wooden planks with a significant overhang at the front.
